title: Path traversal check only rejects literal `..` segments but does not normalise the path before joining, allowing encoded or OS-specific bypass on Windows.
description: The check `if (path.includes('..'))` catches URL-decoded `..` sequences because `decodeURIComponent` is called first (line 33). However, on Windows `normalize()` and `join()` from `node:path` resolve backslash-separated components, so a URL containing percent-encoded backslashes (`%5c..%5c`) would survive the `..` check but might resolve to a parent directory after `join(ROOT, ...parts)`. Since this server is local-dev only and bound to localhost by default with Bun, the practical risk is very low, but the defence-in-depth fix (checking the resolved path starts with ROOT after join) is straightforward.

title: 404 response body echoes the user-controlled path components, which leaks filesystem structure in error messages.
description: When a file is not found, the server responds with `'not found: ' + parts.join('/')`. The `parts` array is derived from the decoded URL pathname. While this is a local dev server and the information disclosed (the requested path) is already known to the requester, reflecting user input in responses is a minor bad-practice worth noting even in dev context.

title: Hardcoded absolute Windows path to a personal photos directory is embedded in source.
description: The ORF path `c:\995\2026-01-09 Birthday at Cederberg\P1100085.ORF` encodes personal photo library structure. This is a dev script committed to the repo, meaning the directory structure of the developer's personal files is disclosed in source history. Not a security vulnerability but a privacy note worth considering if the repo is ever made public.
code_excerpt: const ORF = String.raw`c:\995\2026-01-09 Birthday at Cederberg\P1100085.ORF`;
